hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if systems-optimizer state policy must explicitly require XR `severity_baseline` alignment, so the machine contract cannot silently claim XR severity locking while its retained policy omits that law.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` already reported `ratchet_contract.xr_severity_baseline_required: true` and rejected XR cards whose `severity_baseline` drifted from `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.severity_counts`, but `CLAW/control-plane/system-optimizer/state.json.policy` still omitted `require_xr_severity_baseline`. That left the machine policy under-declared even while the verifier summary claimed the severity lock. After the ratchet, the verifier fails any systems-optimizer state that omits `require_xr_severity_baseline`, and the retained state/backlog/hypothesis surfaces declare the same law while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if systems-optimizer verification fails when retained state policy omits explicit XR `severity_baseline` alignment, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR severity locking must be declared in systems-optimizer state policy, not just enforced inside card validation or verifier summaries; otherwise the machine-level contract can drift from retained XR backlog law.
